questions.subject: IOQM
questions.q: The sides of a triangle are $x, 2 x+1$ and $x+2$ for some positive rational number $x$. If one angle of the triangle is $60^{\circ}$, what is the perimeter of the triangle?
questions.solution:
$x < x+2 < 2 x+1$ for existence triangle also triangle is non-equilateral.

Hence $\cos 60^{\circ}=\frac{x^2+(2 x+1)^2-(x+2)^2}{2 x(2 x+1)}$
$\Rightarrow x=\frac{3}{2}$
$\therefore \quad$ Sides are $\frac{3}{2}, \frac{7}{2}, 4$
$\Rightarrow$ Perimeter $=9$

questions.subject: IOQM
questions.q: $a, b, c$ are positive real numbers such that $a^2+b^2 =c^2$ and $a b=c$. Determine the value of $\left|\frac{(a+b+c)(a+b-c)(b+c-a)(c+a-b)}{c^2}\right|$.
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned}
& a^2+b^2=c^2, a b=c \\
& (a+b)^2=a^2+b^2+2 a b=c^2+2 c \\
& (a+b)^2-c^2=2 c ...(i)\\
& (a-b)^2=c^2-2 c \Rightarrow c^2-(a-b)^2=2 c ...(ii)
\end{aligned}$

Given expression is
$\frac{\left((a+b)^2-c^2\right)\left(c^2-(a-b)^2\right)}{c^2}=\frac{2 c \times 2 c}{c^2}=4$

questions.subject: IOQM
questions.q: Let $A B C$ be an isosceles triangle with $A B=A C$ and incentre $I$. If $A I=3$ and the distance from $I$ to $B C$ is 2 , what is the square of the length of $B C$ ?
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & I M=r=2 \\ & I A=\frac{r}{\sin \frac{A}{2}} \\ & \sin \frac{A}{2}=\frac{r}{I A}=\frac{2}{3} \\ & \tan \frac{A}{2}=\frac{2}{\sqrt{5}}=\frac{B M}{A M} \\ & \frac{2}{\sqrt{5}}=\frac{a}{5} \\ & B C=a=4 \sqrt{5} \\ & B C^2=80\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: IOQM
questions.q: The sides $x$ and $y$ of a scalene triangle satisfy $x+\frac{2 \Delta}{x}=y+\frac{2 \Delta}{y}$, where $\Delta$ is the area of the triangle. If $x=60, y=63$, what is the length of the largest side of the triangle?

questions.subject: IOQM
questions.q: Let $A B C$ be a triangle with $\angle B A C=90^{\circ}$ and $D$ be the point on the side $B C$ such that $A D \perp B C$. Let $r, r_1$, and $r_2$ be the inradii of triangles $A B C, A B D$ and $A C D$, respectively. If $r, r_1$, and $r_2$ are positive integers and one of them is 5 , find the largest possible value of $r+r_1+r_2$.
questions.solution:
$\because \triangle A B D \sim \triangle A C D \sim \triangle A B C$
And hypotenuse of these triangles follow the Pythagoras theorem. So $r_1^2+r_2^2=r^2$ If $r_1, r_2, r \in N$ and least is 5 , then the other two must be 12 and 13 . $\left(r+r_1+r_2\right)_{\max }=30$

questions.subject: IOQM
questions.q: Two sides of a regular polygon with $n$ sides, when extended, meet at an angle of $28^{\circ}$. What is the smallest possible value of $n$ ?
questions.solution:
Let the sides extended pass through the vertices which on joining the centre makes angle $\frac{k(2 \pi)}{n}$
$\begin{aligned}
& \therefore \quad 28+\left(\frac{n-2}{n}\right) \pi+\frac{2 k \pi}{n}=360^{\circ} \\
& \Rightarrow \quad \frac{(2 k-2) \pi}{n}=152^{\circ} \\
& \Rightarrow \frac{(k-1) \pi}{n}=76^{\circ} \\
& \Rightarrow n=\frac{(k-1) 45}{19}
\end{aligned}$

For minimum $k=20$ and $n=45$

questions.subject: IOQM
questions.q:
Let $A B C$ be a triangle with $A B=5, A C=4, B C=6$. The internal angle bisector of $C$ intersects the side $A B$ at $D$. Points $M$ and $N$ are taken on sides $B C$ and $A C$, respectively, such that $D M \| A C$ and $D N \| B C$.

If $(M N)^2=\frac{p}{q}$ where $p$ and $q$ are relatively prime positive integers then what is the sum of the digits of $|p-q| ?$
questions.solution: $\therefore \quad \frac{A D}{B D}=\frac{A C}{B C}=\frac{2}{3}=\frac{C M}{B M}=\frac{A N}{C N}$ So, $C M=\frac{2}{5} \times 6=\frac{12}{5}$ and, $C M=\frac{3}{5} \times 4=\frac{12}{5}$ $\begin{aligned} & \cos C=\frac{B C^2+A C^2-A B^2}{2 B C \cdot A C}=\frac{C M^2+C M^2-M N^2}{2 C N \cdot C M} \\ & \Rightarrow \quad \frac{6^2+4^2-5^2}{2 \cdot 4 \cdot 6}=\frac{\left(\frac{12}{5}\right)^2+\left(\frac{12}{5}\right)^2-M N^2}{2 \cdot\left(\frac{12}{5}\right)\left(\frac{12}{5}\right)} \\ & \Rightarrow \quad M N^2=\frac{126}{25}=\frac{p}{q} \end{aligned}$ Sum of digits of $|p-q|=2$

questions.subject: IOQM
questions.q: Let $A B C$ be an acute-angled triangle and P be a point in its interior. Let $P_A, P_B$, and $P_C$ be the images of $P$ under reflection in the sides $B C, C A$ and $A B$, respectively. If $P$ is the orthocentre of the triangle $P_A P_B P_C$ and if the largest angle of the triangle that can be formed by the line segments $P A$, $P B$, and $P C$ is $x^{\circ}$; determine the value of $x$.
questions.solution: $A B$ is $\perp$ bisector $P P_C \& A C$ is $\perp$ bisector of $P P_B$. $A$ is point of intersection of $\perp$ bisectors $A$ is circumcentre of $\triangle P P_B P_C$ $\text { Circumradius }=C R\left(\Delta P P_B P_C\right)=P A$ Similarly, $C R\left(\Delta P P_C P_A\right)=P B$ $\& C R\left(\Delta P P_A P_B\right)=P C$ But $C R\left(\Delta P P_A P_B\right)=C R\left(\Delta P P_B P_C\right)=C R\left(\Delta P P_C P_A\right)= C R\left(\Delta P_A P_B P_C\right)$ $P C=P A=P B=C R\left(\Delta P_A P_B P_C\right)$ $P A=P C \Rightarrow$ Required triangle is equilateral triangle $\Rightarrow x=60^{\circ}$

questions.subject: IOQM
questions.q: The incircle $\Gamma$ of a scalene triangle $A B C$ touches $B C$ at $D, C A$ at $E$ and $A B$ at $F$. Let $r_A$ be the radius of the circle inside $A B C$ which is tangent to $\Gamma$ and the sides $A B$ and $A C$. Define $r_B$ and $r_C$ similarly. If $r_A=16, r_B=25$ and $r_C=36$, determine the radius of $\Gamma$.
questions.solution:
Let inradius of $\triangle A B C$ be $r$
$\text { In } \triangle I D C: I C=r \operatorname{cosec} \frac{C}{2}$ and $I_1 C=36 \operatorname{cosec} \frac{C}{2}$ $\begin{aligned} & \therefore I_1=r \operatorname{cosec} \frac{C}{2}-36 \operatorname{cosec} \frac{C}{2}=36+r \\ & \Rightarrow \frac{r-36}{r+36}=\sin \frac{C}{2} \end{aligned}$ Similarly $\frac{r-25}{r+25}=\sin \frac{B}{2}$ and $\frac{r-16}{r+16}=\sin \frac{A}{2}$ $\begin{aligned} & \because \quad \sin \frac{A}{2}=\sin \frac{\pi-(B+C)}{2} \\ & \therefore \quad \sin \frac{A}{2}=\cos \frac{B}{2} \cos \frac{C}{2}-\sin \frac{B}{2} \sin \frac{C}{2} \end{aligned}$ $\begin{aligned} \Rightarrow & \frac{r-16}{r+16}=\sqrt{1-\left(\frac{r-25}{r+25}\right)^2} \sqrt{1-\left(\frac{r-36}{r+36}\right)^2} \\ & -\frac{r-25}{r+25} \cdot \frac{r-36}{r+36} \\ \Rightarrow & \frac{r-16}{r+16}=\frac{10 \sqrt{r}}{r+25} \cdot \frac{12 \sqrt{r}}{r+36}-\frac{(r-25)(r-36)}{(r+25)(r+36)} \\ \Rightarrow & (r-16)(r+25)(r+36)=(r+16)(120 r- \\ & \left.\left(r^2-61 r+900\right)\right) \\ \Rightarrow & r\left(r^2+61 r+900-181 r+900+r^2\right)=16\left(r^2+61 r\right. \\ & \left.+900+181 r-900-r^2\right) \\ \Rightarrow & 2 r^2-120 r+1800=16 \times 242 \\ \Rightarrow & r^2-60 r+900=1936 \\ \Rightarrow & r^2-60 r-1036=0 \\ \Rightarrow & (r+14)(r-74)=0 \\ \therefore & r=74\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: IOQM
questions.q: In a triangle $A B C$, the median $A D$ divides $\angle B A C$ in the ratio $1: 2$. Extend $A D$ to $E$ such that $E B$ is perpendicular $A B$. Given that $B E=3, B A=4$, find the integer nearest to $B C^2$.
questions.solution: Here, $D$ is mid-point of $B C$, hence $B D: C D=1: 1$ $\angle A B E=90^{\circ}$ Let $\angle B A D=\theta$, then $\angle C A D=2 \theta$ $\therefore \quad \tan \theta=\frac{3}{4}, \text { and } \tan 2 \theta=\frac{24}{7}$ Now, using $\cot m-n$ theorem in $\triangle A B C$ $\begin{aligned} 2 \cot \alpha & =\cot \theta-\cot 2 \theta \\ \Rightarrow \quad \cot \alpha & =\frac{25}{48} \end{aligned}$ Now, using sine rule in $\triangle A B D$, we get $\begin{aligned} & \frac{B D}{4}=\frac{\sin \theta}{\sin (\pi-\alpha)} \\ \therefore & B D=\frac{4 \times 3 \sqrt{25^2+48^2}}{5 \times 48} \end{aligned}$ So, $4 B D^2=B C^2=\frac{25^2+48^2}{100}=29.29$ Nearest integer is 29 .

questions.subject: IOQM
questions.q:
Let $x, y, z$ be positive real number such that $x^2+y^2=49, y^2+y z+z^2=36$ and $x^2+\sqrt{3} x z+z^2=25$.
If the value of $2 x y+\sqrt{3} y z+z x$ can be written as $p \sqrt{q}$ where $p, q$ are integers and $q$ is not divisible by square of any prime number, find $\mathrm{p}+\mathrm{q}$.
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & x^2+y^2=49 \\ & \frac{x^2+y^2-49}{2 x y}=0 \\ & \cos A=0, A=90^{\circ} \\ & \sin \text { in } \cos B=\frac{y^2+z^2-36}{2 y z}=-\frac{1}{2}, B=120^{\circ} \\ & \cos C=\frac{z^2+x^2-25}{2 x z}=-\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}, C=150^{\circ} \end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned} & \text { Area of } \mathrm{PQR}=\sqrt{9 \times 4 \times 3 \times 2}=6 \sqrt{6} \\ & 2 \mathrm{xy}+\sqrt{3} \mathrm{yz}+\mathrm{zx}=4 \text { Area of } \mathrm{PQR} \\ & =4 \times 6 \sqrt{6}=24 \sqrt{6} \\ & 24+6=30\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: IOQM
questions.q: Let $A B C$ be a triangle let $D$ be a point on the segment $B C$ such that $A D=B C$. Suppose $\angle C A D=x^{\circ}, \angle A B C=y^{\circ}$ and $\angle A C B=z^{\circ}$ and $x, y, z$ are in an arithmetic progression in that order where the first term and the common difference are positive integers. Find the largest possible value of $\angle A B C$ in degrees.
questions.solution:
$\frac{A D}{\sin y}=\frac{B D}{\sin (x+y+z)}$
$\frac{A D}{\sin z}=\frac{C D}{\sin x}$ $\begin{aligned} & \frac{\sin (x+y+z)}{\sin y}+\frac{\sin x}{\sin z}=1 \\ & \frac{\sin (3 y)}{\sin y}+\frac{\sin (y-d)}{\sin (y+d)}=1 \\ & \frac{\sin (y-d)}{\sin (y+d)}=-2 \cos 2 y \\ & \sin (3 y+d)=0 \Rightarrow 3 y+d=180^{\circ}, 360^{\circ} \\ & y \& d \text { are integers } \Rightarrow d \text { is multiple of } 3 \\ & \text { If } 3 y+d=180^{\circ} \Rightarrow y_{\max }=59^{\circ} \\ & 3 y+d=360^{\circ} \Rightarrow y_{\max }=119^{\circ} \text { (if } y \text { is obtuse, then } z \text { must be acute) } \\ & \text { Only } 1^{\text {st }} \text { case is possible } y_{\max }=59^{\circ}\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: IOQM
questions.q: Let $A B C$ be an equilateral triangle with side length 10. $A$ square $P Q R S$ is inscribed in it, with $P$ on $A B$, $Q, R$ on $B C$ and $S$ on $A C$. If the area of the square $P Q R S$ is $m+n \sqrt{k}$ where $m, n$ are integers and $k$ is a prime number then determine the value of $\sqrt{\frac{m+n}{k^2}}$
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \therefore a+2 x=10 \\ & \text { also } \tan 60^{\circ}=\frac{a}{x} \\ & \Rightarrow x=\frac{a}{\sqrt{3}} \\ & \therefore a=\frac{10 \sqrt{3}}{2+\sqrt{3}} \\ & \text { Area of square }=a^2=300(7-4 \sqrt{3}) \\ & \therefore m=2100, n=-1200, k=3 \\ & \sqrt{\frac{m+n}{k^2}}=\sqrt{\frac{900}{9}}=10\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: IOQM
questions.q: The ex-radii of a triangle are $10 \frac{1}{2}, 12$ and 14 . If the sides of the triangle are the roots of the cubic $x^3-p x^2+q x-r=0$, where $p, q, r$ are integers, find the integer nearest to $\sqrt{p+q+r}$.
questions.solution:
$\because \quad r_1=\frac{\Delta}{S-a}=\frac{21}{2}, r_2=\frac{\Delta}{S-b}=12$
and $r_3=\frac{\Delta}{S-c}=14$
On solving above equations, we get
$a=13, b=14, c=15$
Let $f(x)=x^3-p x^2+q x-r$
$=(x-13)(x-14)(x-15)$
$f(-1)=-1-p-q-r$
$=(-14)(-15)(-16)$
$\therefore \quad p+q+r=14 \cdot 15 \cdot 16-1$
$\therefore \quad \sqrt{p+q+r}=\sqrt{3364} \approx 58$

questions.subject: IOQM
questions.q: In a trapezoid $A B C D$, the internal bisector of angle $A$ intersects the base $B C$ (or its extension) at the point $E$. Inscribed in the triangle $A B E$ is a circle touching the side $A B$ at $M$ and side $B E$ at the point $P$. Find the angle $D A E$ in degrees, if $A B: M P=2$.
questions.solution: $A D \| B C$ Given, $P M=\frac{x+y}{2}$ $\begin{aligned} & \angle B A E=\angle A E B=\theta \\ \Rightarrow \quad & x=z \end{aligned}$ By symmetry $M$ is mid-point of $B A$ $\begin{array}{ll} \therefore & \frac{x+y}{2}=y \Rightarrow x=y \\ \therefore & \angle B=60^{\circ} \Rightarrow \angle A=60^{\circ} \end{array}$

questions.subject: IOQM
questions.q: In a triangle $A B C$, let $E$ be the midpoint of $A C$ and $F$ be the midpoint of $A B$. The medians $B E$ and $C F$ intersect at $G$. Let $Y$ and $Z$ be the midpoints of $B E$ and $C F$ respectively. If the area of triangle $A B C$ is 480 , find the area of triangle $C Y Z$.
questions.solution: Let $B E=6 x$ $C F=6 y$ $\frac{\operatorname{area}(G Y Z)}{\operatorname{area}(G B C)}=\left(\frac{1}{4}\right)^2$ $\operatorname{Area}(G B C)=16($ area of $G Y Z)$ area of $G B C=\frac{1}{3}$ of $\operatorname{area}(A B C)=\frac{480}{3}=160$ area of $G Y Z=10$

questions.subject: IOQM
questions.q: In a triangle $A B C, \angle B A C=90^{\circ}$. Let $D$ be the point on $B C$ such that $A B+B D=A C+C D$. Suppose $B D: D C=2: 1$. If $\frac{A C}{A B}=\frac{m+\sqrt{p}}{n}$, where $m, n$ are relatively prime positive integers and $p$ is a prime number, determine the value of $m+n+p$.
questions.solution:
$A B+B D=A C+C D$
$\begin{aligned} & y+2 x=A C+x \\ & \Rightarrow \quad A C=x+y \\ & \Rightarrow \quad(x+y)^2+y^2=(3 x)^2 \\ & \Rightarrow \quad x^2+2 y^2+2 x y=9 x^2 \\ & \Rightarrow \quad 8 x^2-2 y^2-2 x y=0 \\ & \Rightarrow \quad 4 a^2-a-1=0 \\ & {\left[\begin{array}{ll}\therefore & a=\frac{x}{y}\end{array}\right]} \\ & \Rightarrow \quad a=\frac{1 \pm \sqrt{1+16}}{8} \\ & \Rightarrow \quad a=\frac{1 \pm \sqrt{17}}{8} \\ & \Rightarrow \quad \frac{A C}{A B}=\frac{x}{y}+1=a+1 \\ & \Rightarrow \quad \frac{A C}{A B}=\frac{1+\sqrt{17}}{8}+1 \\ & \Rightarrow \quad \frac{A C}{A B}=\frac{9+\sqrt{17}}{8} \\ & \Rightarrow \quad m+n+p=34\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: IOQM
questions.q: Let $A B C$ be a right-angled triangle with $\angle B=90^{\circ}$. Let the length of the altitude $B D$ be equal to 12 . What is the minimum possible length of $A C$, given that $A C$ and the perimeter of triangle $A B C$ are integers?
questions.solution:
$a c=12 b$ Let $a+c=I \quad l \in$ integer $a^2+c^2=R-24 b=b^2$
$\begin{aligned} & D=\geq 0 \\ & I^2-48 b \geq 0 \\ & b^2+24 I \geq 0 \quad b \geq 24 \\ & \text { Let } b=k-12 \quad k \geq 36 \\ & \begin{array}{l}R=k^2-144 \\ 144=(k-I)(k+I) \\ \text { For } k=37, I=35 \\ I=25 \text { is minimum value }\end{array}\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: IOQM
questions.q: Let $A B C$ be a triangle with $A B=A C$. Let $D$ be a point on the segment $B C$ such that $B D=48 \frac{1}{61}$ and $D C=61$. Let $E$ be a point on $A D$ such that $C E$ is perpendicular to $A D$ and $D E=11$. Find $A E$.
questions.solution: Let $B D=48 \frac{1}{61}=\alpha, M$ is mid-point of $B C$. $\begin{aligned} & C D=61=\beta \\ & B M=C M=\frac{\beta+\alpha}{2} \\ & D M=B M-B D=\frac{\beta-\alpha}{2} \end{aligned}$ Let $A E=x, A B=A C=y, A M=h, C E=z$ In $\triangle C D E$, $\begin{aligned} & C E^2=C D^2-D E^2=61^2-11^2=50 \times 72 \\ & C E=60=z ...(i)\\ & \text { In } \triangle A C E, y^2=x^2+60^2 \\ & \quad y^2=x^2+3600 ...(ii) \end{aligned}$ In $\triangle A C M, y^2=h^2+\left(\frac{\beta+\alpha}{2}\right)^2$...(iii) In $\triangle A D M,(x+11)^2=h^2+\left(\frac{\beta-\alpha}{2}\right)^2$...(iv) (ii) \& (iii) $\Rightarrow x^2+3600=h^2+\left(\frac{\beta+\alpha}{2}\right)^2$...(v) $\begin{aligned} & \text { (iv) } \&(\mathrm{v}) \Rightarrow(x+11)^2-x^2-3600=\left(\frac{\beta-\alpha}{2}\right)^2-\left(\frac{\beta+\alpha}{2}\right)^2 \\ & 22 x+121-3600=-\alpha \beta \\ & =-\frac{(48 \times 61)+1}{61} \times 61 \\ & 22 x=3600-121-2929 \\ & x=25=A E\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: IOQM
questions.q: Let ABC be a triangle with sides $51,52,53$. Let $\Omega$ denote the incircle of $\triangle \mathrm{ABC}$. Draw tangents to $\Omega$ which are parallel to the sides of ABC . Let $\mathrm{r}_1, \mathrm{r}_2, \mathrm{r}_3$ be the inradii of the three corner triangles so formed. Find the largest integer that does not exceed $r_1+r_2+r_3$.
questions.solution: Let PQ be one of the required tangents is parallel to BC and meets sides AB and AC at P and Q respectively. Further Let $P Q=x$ and $B C=51$ Now $\triangle \mathrm{ABC}$ is similar to $\triangle \mathrm{APQ}$ so $\frac{\mathrm{x}}{\mathrm{a}}=\frac{\mathrm{r}_1}{\mathrm{r}}$ $\begin{aligned} \text{Similarly} & \frac{y}{6}=\frac{r_2}{r} \\ \text{Hence} & \frac{r_1+r_2+r_3}{r}=\frac{x}{a}+\frac{y}{b}+\frac{z}{c} \\ \text{Now} & \frac{x}{a}=\frac{h-2 r}{h} \text { where } h \text { is altitude } \end{aligned}$ $\begin{array}{ll} & \frac{x}{a}=1-\frac{2 \Delta}{s \cdot 2 \Delta}, x=1-\frac{a}{s} \\ & \sum \frac{x}{a}=3-\frac{a+b+c}{s}=1 \\ \text { so } & r_1+r_2+r_3=r \\ \text { Now } & r=\sqrt{\frac{78(27)(26)(25)}{78}} \\ & =\sqrt{\frac{3^3 \cdot 5^2 \cdot 2.13}{2 \cdot 3 \cdot 13}} \\ & =\sqrt{3^2 \cdot 5^2}=15\end{array}$

questions.subject: IOQM
questions.q: Let ABC be a triangle and let $\Omega$ be its circumcircle. The internal bisectors of angles A, B and C intersect $\Omega$ at $\mathrm{A}_1, \mathrm{~B}_1$ and $\mathrm{C}_1$ respectively and the internal bisectors of angles $\mathrm{A}_1, \mathrm{~B}_1$ and $\mathrm{C}_1$ of the triangle $\mathrm{A}_1 \mathrm{~B}_1 \mathrm{C}_1$ intersect $\Omega$ at $\mathrm{A}_2, \mathrm{~B}_2$ and $\mathrm{C}_2$, respectively. If the smallest angle of triangle ABC is $40^{\circ}$, what is the magnitude of the smallest angle of triangle $\mathrm{A}_2 \mathrm{~B}_2 \mathrm{C}_2$ in degrees?
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & \angle \mathrm{A}_1 \mathrm{~B}_1 \mathrm{C}_1=\frac{\pi}{2}-\frac{\angle \mathrm{ABC}}{2} \\ & \angle \mathrm{~A}_1 \mathrm{C}_1 \mathrm{~B}_1=\frac{\pi}{2}-\frac{\angle \mathrm{ACB}}{2} \\ & \angle \mathrm{~B}_1 \mathrm{~A}_1 \mathrm{C}_1=\frac{\pi}{2}-\frac{\angle \mathrm{BAC}}{2} \\ & \angle \mathrm{~A}_2 \mathrm{~B}_2 \mathrm{C}_2=\frac{\pi}{2}-\frac{\left(\frac{\pi}{2}-\frac{\angle \mathrm{ABC}}{2}\right)}{2} \\ & =\frac{\pi}{4}+\frac{\angle \mathrm{ABC}}{4}\end{aligned}$
similarly $\angle \mathrm{A}_2 \mathrm{C}_2 \mathrm{~B}_2=\frac{\pi}{4}+\frac{\angle \mathrm{ACB}}{4}$
and $\angle \mathrm{B}_2 \mathrm{~A}_2 \mathrm{C}_2=\frac{\pi}{4}+\frac{\angle \mathrm{BAC}}{4}$
$\Rightarrow$ smaller angle of $\Delta A_2 B_2 C_2$ is $45^{\circ}+\left(\frac{40^{\circ}}{4}\right)=55^{\circ}$

questions.subject: IOQM
questions.q: In a triangle ABC , the median AD (with D on BC ) and the angle bisector BE (with E on AC ) are perpendicular to each other. If $\mathrm{AD}=7$ and $\mathrm{BE}=9$, find the integer nearest to the area of triangle $A B C$.
questions.solution: Let $A D$ and $B E$ meet at $F$ Now $\angle \mathrm{ABF}=\angle \mathrm{FBD}=\frac{\mathrm{B}}{2}$ and $\angle \mathrm{AFB}=\angle \mathrm{BFD}=30^{\circ}$ and BF is common to triangles ABF and BFD hence the two triangles are Cengruent so $\mathrm{AF}=\mathrm{FD}=\frac{7}{2}$ Also, $\mathrm{AB}=\mathrm{BD}, \Rightarrow \mathrm{AB}: \mathrm{BC}=1: 2$ $\Rightarrow \mathrm{AE}: \mathrm{EC}=1: 2$ So area of triangle $A B C=3$ (Area of $\triangle A B E$ ) $\begin{aligned} & =3\left(\frac{1}{2} \times \mathrm{AF} \times \mathrm{BE}\right) \\ & =\frac{3}{2} \times \frac{7}{2} \times 9=\frac{189}{4}=47.25 \end{aligned}$ Hence nearest integer = 47

questions.subject: IOQM
questions.q: In a quadrilateral $A B C D$, it is given that $A B=A D=13, B C=C D=20, B D=24$. If $r$ is the radius of the circle inscribable in the quadrilateral, then what is the integer closest to $r$ ?
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} \text { Area of } A B C D & =\text { Area of } \triangle A B D+\text { Area of } \triangle B C D \\ & =\sqrt{25 \times 12 \times 12 \times 1}+\sqrt{32 \times 8 \times 12 \times 12} \\ & =60+192=252 \\ \text { Inradius(r) } & =\frac{\text { Area }}{\text { semi-perimeter }}=\frac{252}{33}=\frac{84}{11}=7 . \overline{64} \end{aligned}$ Hence integer nearest to $r$ is 8.

questions.subject: IOQM
questions.q: If 15 and 9 are lengths of two medians of a triangle, what is the maximum possible area of the triangle to the nearest integer?
questions.solution:
Area of $\triangle \mathrm{BDG}$
$\begin{aligned} & \text { S }=\frac{11+x}{2} \\ & \begin{aligned} \text { Area }= & \sqrt{\frac{11+x}{2}\left(\frac{11+x}{2}-x\right)\left(\frac{11+x}{2}-5\right)\left(\frac{11+x}{2}-6\right)} \\ & =\sqrt{\frac{11+x}{2}\left(\frac{11-x}{2}\right)\left(\frac{x+1}{2}\right)\left(\frac{x-1}{2}\right)} \\ & =\sqrt{\frac{\left(121-x^2\right)\left(x^2-1\right)}{16}}\end{aligned} \\ & \text { Let } \mathrm{y}=\frac{\left(121-\mathrm{x}^2\right)\left(\mathrm{x}^2-1\right)}{16} \\ & \text { For } \mathrm{max} \text { area } \frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{dx}}=0 \\ & -2 \mathrm{x}\left(\mathrm{x}^2-1\right)+\left(121-\mathrm{x}^2\right) 2 \mathrm{x}=0 \\ & -\mathrm{x}^2+1+121-\mathrm{x}^2=0\end{aligned}$ $\begin{aligned} & 2 x^2=122 \\ & x^2=61 \\ & \text { Area of } \Delta \mathrm{BDG}=\sqrt{\frac{(121-61)(61-1)}{16}} \\ & =\sqrt{\frac{60 \times 60}{16}}=\frac{60}{4}=15 \end{aligned}$ Area of $\triangle \mathrm{ABC}=6 \times 15=90$

questions.subject: IOQM
questions.q: In a triangle ABC , right-angled at A , the altitude through A and the internal bisector of $\angle \mathrm{A}$ have lengths 3 and 4, respectively. Find the length of the median through A .
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \angle \mathrm{CAE}=45^{\circ}=\angle \mathrm{BAE} \\ & \mathrm{AD}=3\end{aligned}$ Let $B C=a, C A=b, A B=c$ $\begin{aligned} & \frac{1}{2} b c=\frac{1}{2} \cdot a \cdot 3 \Rightarrow b c=3 a \\ & \frac{2 b c}{b+c} \cos \frac{A}{2}=4 \Rightarrow \frac{6 a}{b+c} \cdot \frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}=4 \\ & \Rightarrow 2 \sqrt{2}(b+c)=3 a \\ & \Rightarrow 8\left(b^2+c^2+2 b c\right)=9 a^2 \\ & \Rightarrow 8\left(a^2+6 a\right)=9 a^2 \\ & \Rightarrow 48 a=a^2 \Rightarrow a=48 \end{aligned}$ So $\mathrm{AF}=\frac{\mathrm{a}}{2}=24$

questions.subject: IOQM
questions.q: In a triangle $A B C$, the median from $B$ to $C A$ is perpendicular to the median from $C$ to $A B$. If the median from $A$ to $B C$ is 30 , determine $\left(B C^2+C A^2+A B^2\right) / 100$.
questions.solution: Let G be centroid, $\mathrm{D}, \mathrm{E}, \mathrm{F}$ be mid-points of $\mathrm{BC}, \mathrm{CA}, \mathrm{AB}$ \& M be mid-point of FE . Let $\mathrm{BE}=3 \mathrm{x}, \mathrm{CF}=3 \mathrm{y}$, given $\mathrm{AD}=30$ Hence $\mathrm{AM}=5, \mathrm{GM}=5, \mathrm{GD}=10, \mathrm{BG}=2 \mathrm{x}, \mathrm{GE}=\mathrm{x}, \mathrm{CG}=2 \mathrm{y}, \mathrm{GF}=\mathrm{y}$ Now $D$ is mid-point of hypotenuse of right angle triangle $B G C$. So $D$ is circum centre of the triangle. $\mathrm{SO} \mathrm{BD}=\mathrm{GD}=10 \Rightarrow \mathrm{BC}=20$ Hence $4\left(x^2+y^2\right)=400 \Rightarrow x^2+y^2=100$ Now $9\left(x^2+y^2\right)=\frac{1}{4}\left\{2 B C^2+2 A B^2-A C^2+2 B C^2+2 A C^2-A B^2\right\}$ $\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow 900 \times 4=4 B C^2+A B^2+A C^2 \\ & \Rightarrow A B^2+A C^2=3600-1600=2000 \end{aligned}$ Hence $\frac{A B^2+B C^2+C A^2}{100}=\frac{2400}{100}=24$

questions.subject: IOQM
questions.q: The centre of the circle passing through the midpoints of the sides of an isosceles triangle $A B C$ lies on the circumcircle of triangle $A B C$. If the larger angle of triangle $A B C$ is $\alpha^0$ and the smaller one $\beta^{\circ}$ then what is the value of $\alpha-\beta$ ?
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & A+2 B=180 \\ & \frac{A}{2}=90-B \\ & 2 x+2 B=180 \\ & x=90-B \\ & x=\frac{A}{2} \\ & \Rightarrow O P \perp B C \end{aligned}$ $\therefore \mathrm{O}$ and A coincide $\begin{aligned} & \therefore \angle \mathrm{ORP}=\angle \mathrm{OPR}=\angle \mathrm{RAP}=60^{\circ} \\ & \therefore \alpha=120^{\circ} \beta=30^{\circ} \\ & \alpha-\beta=90^{\circ} \end{aligned}$ By drawing properly we get

questions.subject: IOQM
questions.q: Let ABC be an isosceles triangle with $\mathrm{AB}=\mathrm{BC}$. A trisector of $\angle \mathrm{B}$ meets AC at D . If $\mathrm{AB}, \mathrm{AC}$ and $B D$ are integers and $A B-B D=3$, find $A C$.
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \frac{I_1}{\sin \left(\frac{\pi}{2}+\theta\right)}=\frac{I_3}{\sin \left(\frac{\pi}{2}-3 \theta\right)} \\ & \Rightarrow \text { put } I_3=I_1-3 \\ & \Rightarrow I_1=\frac{3}{4} \operatorname{cosec}^2 \theta \\ & \text { If } \operatorname{cosec}^2 \theta=36 \\ & \text { i.e. } \operatorname{cosec}^2 \theta=6\end{aligned}$ $\begin{aligned} & \text { i.e. } \sin \theta=\frac{1}{6} \\ & I_1=\frac{3}{4} \times 36=27 \\ & I_3=24 \\ & \frac{I_1}{\sin \left(\frac{\pi}{2}-\theta\right)}=\frac{D C}{\sin 4 \theta} \& \frac{I_1}{\sin \left(\frac{\pi}{2}+\theta\right)}=\frac{A D}{\sin 2 \theta} \\ & \Rightarrow I_2=\frac{I_1 \sin 4 \theta}{\cos \theta}+\frac{I_1 \sin 2 \theta}{\cos \theta} \\ & \Rightarrow I_2=I_1 \times 2 \times \sin 3 \theta\end{aligned}$ $\begin{aligned} \Rightarrow & \frac{3}{4} \operatorname{cosec}^2 \theta \times 2 \times \sin 3 \theta \\ \Rightarrow & \frac{3}{2}\left(\frac{3}{\sin \theta}-4 \sin \theta\right) \\ \mathrm{I}_2 & =\frac{3}{2}\left(3 \times 6-\frac{4}{6}\right) \\ & =\frac{3}{2}\left(18-\frac{2}{3}\right) \\ & =\frac{3}{2}\left(\frac{54-2}{3}\right) \\ & =\frac{3}{2} \times \frac{52}{3} \\ \mathrm{I}_2 & =26\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: IOQM
questions.q: In a triangle ABC , it is known that $\angle \mathrm{A}=100^{\circ}$ and $\mathrm{AB}=\mathrm{AC}$. The internal angle bisector BD has length 20 units. Find the length of BC to the nearest integer, given that $\sin 10^{\circ} \approx 0.174$.
questions.solution:
$\triangle \mathrm{BCD}$, by sine rule
$\begin{aligned} & \frac{B C}{120^{\circ}}=\frac{B D}{\sin 40^{\circ}}=\frac{20}{\sin 40^{\circ}} \\ & \therefore B C=\frac{10 \sqrt{3}}{\sin 40^{\circ}} \end{aligned}$ Given, $\cos 80^{\circ}\left(\sin 10^{\circ}\right) \approx 0.174$ $\sin 40^{\circ} \approx \sqrt{\frac{1-\cos 80^{\circ}}{2}} \approx 0.643$ $\therefore$ Nearest integer to $\mathrm{BC}=27$

questions.subject: IOQM
questions.q: Triangles ABC and DEF are such that $\angle \mathrm{A}=\angle \mathrm{D}, \mathrm{AB}=\mathrm{DE}=17, \mathrm{BC}=\mathrm{EF}=10$ and $\mathrm{AC}-\mathrm{DF}=12$. What is $\mathrm{AC}+\mathrm{DF}$ ?
questions.solution:
Let $A$ coincides with $D, B$ coincides with $E$. With $E(B)$ as centre draw a circle with radius 10 intersecting the line, making angle $\theta=\angle \mathrm{A}$ with AB , at $\mathrm{F} \& \mathrm{C}$.
Let $N$ be the foot of perpendicular from $B(E)$ to $C F$ So, $\mathrm{BM}=8$ Hence, $\mathrm{AM}=\sqrt{17^2-8^2}=\sqrt{(25)(9)}=15$ Hence, $A F=15-6=9$ \& $A C=15+6=21$ So, $\mathrm{AC}+\mathrm{DF}=30$

questions.subject: IOQM
questions.q: Let $A B C$ be an acute-angled triangle and let $H$ be its orthocentre. Let $G_1, G_2$ and $G_3$ be the centroids of the triangles $\mathrm{HBC}, \mathrm{HCA}$ and HAB respectively. If the area of triangle $\mathrm{G}_1 \mathrm{G}_2 \mathrm{G}_3$ is 7 units, what is the area of triangle $A B C$ ?
questions.solution: $A B=2 D E$....(i) In $\Delta \mathrm{H} \mathrm{G}_1 \mathrm{G}_2 \quad \& \quad \Delta \mathrm{HDE}$ $\frac{\mathrm{HG}_1}{\mathrm{HD}}=\frac{\mathrm{G}_1 \mathrm{G}_2}{\mathrm{DE}}=\frac{2}{3}$ $\mathrm{G}_1 \mathrm{G}_2=\frac{2}{3} \mathrm{DE}=\frac{2}{3}\left(\frac{\mathrm{AB}}{2}\right)=\frac{\mathrm{AB}}{3}$ Becomes $\Delta \mathrm{G}_1 \mathrm{G}_2 \mathrm{G}_3 \sim \Delta \mathrm{ABC}$ $\Rightarrow \frac{\text { Area of } \Delta A B C}{\text { Area of } \Delta G_1 G_2 G_3}=\frac{(A B)^2}{\left(G_1 G_2\right)^2}=\left(\frac{A B}{G_1 G_2}\right)^2=\left(\frac{3}{1}\right)^2$ $\Rightarrow$ Area of $\triangle \mathrm{ABC}=9 \times\left(\right.$ Area of $\left.\triangle \mathrm{G}_1 \mathrm{G}_2 \mathrm{G}_3\right)$ $\Rightarrow$ Area of $\triangle \mathrm{ABC}=9 \times 7=63$

questions.subject: IOQM
questions.q: Let D be an interior point of the side BC of a triangle ABC . Let $\mathrm{I}_1$ and $\mathrm{I}_2$ be the incentres of triangles $A B D$ and $A C D$ respectively. Let $A I_1$ and $A I_2$ meet $B C$ in $E$ and $F$ respectively. If $\angle B I_1 E=60^{\circ}$. What is the measure of $\angle C I_2 F$ in degrees?
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \angle \mathrm{BAD}=120^{\circ}-\mathrm{B} \\ & \angle \mathrm{CAD}=\angle \mathrm{A}-\left(120^{\circ}-\mathrm{B}\right) \\ & =\mathrm{A}+\mathrm{B}-120^{\circ} \\ & \angle \mathrm{FAC}=\frac{\mathrm{A}+\mathrm{B}}{2}-60^{\circ} \Rightarrow 90^{\circ}-\frac{\mathrm{C}}{2}-60^{\circ} \Rightarrow 30^{\circ}-\frac{\mathrm{C}}{2} \\ & \angle \mathrm{AFC}=180^{\circ}-\left(\mathrm{C}+30^{\circ}-\frac{\mathrm{C}}{2}\right) \\ & =150-\mathrm{C} / 2 \\ & \angle \mathrm{Cl}_2 \mathrm{~F}=180^{\circ} \mathrm{m}-\left(150^{\circ}-\frac{\mathrm{C}}{2}+\frac{\mathrm{C}}{2}\right)=30^{\circ}\end{aligned}$
